Fixes and tests the pathfinder runner so candidate-specific n_programs settings are actually used.
Documents the invalid first pathfinder artifact and the corrected fixed artifact separately.
Updates the Run 5 training preset to the corrected pathfinder recommendation: p12 base_semantic with category_route_weight 0.1.
Applies run5_capability training defaults through the trainer preset, including MI objective and 2000 warmup steps.
Has automated tests for variant-grid coverage, candidate program-count propagation, MI calculation, ranking rejection logic, preset training kwargs, and trainer preset defaults.

tickets.title: Run 5 real-scale capability validation
acceptance: Runs a local smoke of kaggle/train_best_tac_agentic.py --preset run5_capability and verifies p12 base_semantic, category_route_weight 0.1, MI objective, warmup defaults, and specialization checkpoint wiring.
Adds a regression test proving the Kaggle code bundle imports tac_transformer successfully after zip extraction.
Includes authority.py, capability.py, capability sanity docs, and Run 5 pathfinder docs/scripts in the Kaggle code bundle.
Uploads a private Run 5 code dataset built from the corrected bundle.
Launches the p12/w0.1 Run 5 Kaggle kernel on the dual-T4 path with periodic specialization checkpoints at 2k/5k/10k/20k and final specialization analysis.
Creates an automated heartbeat monitor for status audits, completion artifact pull, ERROR repair, and resume handling.

On completion, inspects final_summary.json, metrics.jsonl, specialization checkpoints, and final specialization artifacts to decide whether Run 5 restores capability and produces functional specialization.
tickets.launch_note: Version 1 pushed on 2026-06-02 after local preset smoke, extracted bundle import regression, wrapper compile, JSON validation, and private code dataset readiness check.
tickets.follow_up_gate: Run 5B optimizer/precision sanity repair before objective or architecture promotion: validate nonzero gradients, avoid fp16 scaler collapse, sanity-check a vanilla baseline on the same trainer path, and require capability recovery before interpreting final specialization and knockout artifacts.

tickets.title: TAC-aware optimizer across training use cases
acceptance: Adds a public TAC optimizer factory that returns checkpoint-compatible torch.optim.AdamW.
Groups trainable parameters exactly once into core, identity, router, memory, and head categories with decay/no-decay subgroups.
Supports per-category learning-rate multipliers while keeping conservative 1.0 defaults for comparability.
Preserves TAC learning-rate multipliers in the Kaggle warmup/cosine scheduler.
Falls back cleanly for vanilla transformer and recurrent baseline models.
Replaces raw AdamW construction across local training, chunked memory, capability/pathfinder research, agentic training, Kaggle training, profiling, and timing experiments.
Exports the optimizer API from tac_transformer and includes it in the Kaggle code bundle.
Documents the grouping contract and integration coverage.
Has automated tests for parameter coverage, no duplicate groups, LR multipliers, no-decay behavior, vanilla fallback, training-step usability, scheduler preservation, and bundle extraction import.

tickets.title: Deferred TAC objective and efficiency research priorities
acceptance: Records that Run 4 does not prove next-token prediction is fundamentally incompatible with TAC.
Frames TAC as learning computational organization rather than only statistical prediction.
Adds a working hypothesis table separating capacity share, routing objective strength, NTP mismatch, and other causes.
Defines escalation criteria for when alternative objectives should become a primary research track.
Documents deferred objective tracks: latent-state prediction, predictive coding, program contrastive learning, route-and-reconstruct, and computation prediction.

Documents a neuroscience-inspired efficiency roadmap covering incremental state evolution, sparse activation, hierarchical update schedules, event-driven retrieval, prediction-error updates, and macro-program formation.
Keeps Run 5 as the active capability test and marks the new tracks as deferred priorities rather than immediate architecture conclusions.

tickets.title: Local TAC objective and efficiency research matrix
acceptance: Adds tested local research utilities for latent-state, predictive-coding, useful program-contrastive, route-reconstruction, and computation-prediction losses.
Adds macro-program repetition/compression analysis for routed program sequences.
Adds a benchmark script that trains objective variants on the hard agentic corpus without touching the active Kaggle run.
Runs a multi-seed local objective matrix comparing NTP, Run 5 MI regularization, and deferred alternative objectives.
Runs local efficiency probes for full updates, serving no-aux mode, disabled content updates, periodic content updates, and event-error updates.
Writes JSON and Markdown artifacts under runs/benchmarks and records a capability/specialization/efficiency verdict in research.md.
Keeps any conclusions local and provisional until Kaggle Run 5 completes.

tickets.title: Content-memory update-frequency isolation research
acceptance: Adds a local benchmark harness that separates context-phase content writes from query/decode-phase content upkeep.
Includes tested schedule helpers for full updates, query-skip updates, no updates, and periodic segmented-context updates.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix across chunked recall tasks without touching the active Kaggle Run 5 kernel.
Reports carry/reset/shuffled value accuracy, content-addressed hit, content gate/reconsolidation metrics, throughput, and update fractions by schedule.
Writes JSON and Markdown artifacts under runs/benchmarks.
Updates research.md, progress.txt, and prd.json with a provisional recommendation for the next content-memory architecture experiment.

tickets.title: Prediction-error content-write threshold sweep
acceptance: Extends the content-update benchmark with event-error context schedules across multiple prediction-loss thresholds.
Adds tested helper logic for first-segment seeding and previous-loss threshold updates.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local threshold matrix across chunked recall tasks without touching the active Kaggle Run 5 kernel.
Reports the A1 gate explicitly: write reduction, context-loss degradation, carry degradation, content hit, and throughput.
Documents the invalid one-token-segment artifact separately from the corrected two-token-segment artifact.
Records an implementation-ready decision: keep query_skip, block segmented event-error writes, and redesign A1 as an in-forward content_write_mask/write_policy experiment.

tickets.title: In-forward prediction-error content-write mask
acceptance: Adds a public content_write_mask path through TACTransformerLM, TACTransformerBlock, and IdentityField without changing default behavior.
Masks content-store cue/value writes inside a full prefill forward instead of segmenting context.
Adds benchmark schedules for full-context prediction-error masks across multiple thresholds.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local masked-threshold matrix across chunked recall tasks.
Reports the A1 gate against the corrected implementation surface: write reduction, context-loss degradation, carry degradation, content hit, and throughput.
Records the decision that raw CE thresholding is not the balanced solution, while content_write_mask remains the right API for future learned or retrieval-aware write gates.
Has automated tests for masked content writes, invalid mask shapes, prediction-error mask generation, and masked full-context benchmark scheduling.

tickets.title: Retrieval-aware content-write mask research
acceptance: Adds retrieval-aware full-context write-mask schedules for top 25%, 50%, and 75% cue/value pair writes.
Implements a tested retrieval_aware_content_write_mask that combines cue loss, value loss, and hidden-state novelty while preserving the first pair.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix across chunked recall tasks without touching the active Kaggle Run 5 kernel.
Reports the A1 gate for retrieval-aware sparse writes: write reduction, context-loss degradation, carry degradation, carried-state advantage, content hit, and throughput.
Tightens the aggregate sparse-write decision gate so degenerate full-update carry cannot pass as a useful sparse-write solution.
Records the decision that retrieval-aware fixed top-k scoring improves the tradeoff over raw CE thresholding but still does not meet the balanced A1 gate.
Defines the next implementation-ready candidate as a learned or calibrated write gate trained against carried-state preservation, cue/value usefulness, and multi-key overwrite risk.

tickets.title: Structural boundary sparse-write oracle
acceptance: Adds a structural_pair_top_4 schedule on the corrected full-prefill content_write_mask path.
Implements and tests structural_pair_content_write_mask for early odd-position cue/value boundary writes.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing full update, query_skip, dense CE masking, retrieval-aware masking, and structural boundary masking.
Reports the sparse context-write gate against A1 criteria: write reduction, context-loss degradation, carry degradation, carried-state advantage, content hit, and throughput.
Demonstrates the first local A1 pass: structural_pair_top_4 writes about 12.9% of pairs, preserves context loss, and improves carry over full update.
Records the decision that the production architecture should learn cue/value boundary and overwrite-risk gating rather than use the task-oracle mask directly.

tickets.title: Calibrated boundary write-gate probe
acceptance: Adds a calibrated_boundary_top_4 schedule on the corrected full-prefill content_write_mask path.
Implements a tiny trained BoundaryWriteGate with feature extraction, supervised calibration against structural sparse-write targets, and top-k mask generation.
Adds focused tests for boundary-gate features, calibration recovery, and schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing full update, query_skip, structural oracle, and calibrated boundary gating.
Reports that calibrated_boundary_top_4 passes aggregate sparsity with 12.9% writes and positive mean carry delta, but regresses multi-key carry.
Records the decision that the production A1 gate must add hidden-state/retrieval and overwrite-risk features plus task-level no-regression gates before promotion.

tickets.title: Ranking-trained boundary write gate
acceptance: Adds a ranked_boundary_top_4 schedule on the corrected full-prefill content_write_mask path.
Implements train_ranked_boundary_write_gate with a top-k margin ranking objective plus BCE stabilizer against structural sparse-write targets.
Adds focused tests for ranked boundary recovery and ranked schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing full update, query_skip, structural oracle, BCE-calibrated boundary gating, and ranking-trained boundary gating.
Reports that ranked_boundary_top_4 is the strongest learned local A1 candidate so far: 12.9% writes, positive aggregate carry delta, and multi-key no-regression versus full update.

Records the decision to promote ranking-trained boundary gating as the next opt-in implementation candidate, while requiring hidden-state/retrieval/overwrite features and multi-hop capability gates before production promotion.

tickets.title: Hybrid ranked sparse-write gate probe
acceptance: Adds a hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 schedule on the corrected full-prefill content_write_mask path.
Implements hybrid boundary features combining position/parity/tokens with token-loss, next-token-loss, hidden-novelty, cue-recurrence, and value-recurrence probe signals.
Implements train_hybrid_ranked_boundary_write_gate with the TAC-102 top-k margin ranking objective over the richer features.
Adds focused tests for hybrid feature extraction, trained recovery, and schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing full update, query_skip, structural oracle, TAC-102 ranked boundary, and hybrid ranked boundary gating.
Reports that hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 is the strongest aggregate learned A1 candidate so far: 12.9% writes, context-loss ratio 1.0021, carry 0.2625, and +0.0847 carry delta versus full update.
Records the decision that hybrid ranked boundary gating is the next opt-in prototype, but production promotion remains blocked by multi-hop no-regression and noisy-key stability requirements.

tickets.title: Sparse write-budget sweep
acceptance: Adds structural, ranked-boundary, and hybrid-ranked top-6/top-8 schedules on the corrected content_write_mask path.
Identifies and fixes schedule-order dependence in learned gate training by adding deterministic schedule-specific RNG seeding.
Adds a focused regression test proving ranked-boundary schedule results are independent of prior learned-gate training.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing top-4/top-6/top-8 sparse write budgets across structural, ranked, and hybrid gates.
Reports that larger write budgets increase content-hit but reduce aggregate carry and introduce multi-key interference.

Records the decision to keep top-4 hybrid/ranked boundary gating as the A1 sparse-write prototype and reject top-6/top-8 as production candidates until a separate multi-hop retrieval-chain mechanism exists.

tickets.title: Two-step retrieval-chain probe
acceptance: Adds query_skip_chain_k2, ranked_boundary_top_4_chain_k2, and hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_chain_k2 benchmark schedules.
Implements memory_chain_steps propagation through schedule evaluation and query readout.
Implements chained_memory_readout that feeds the first memory prediction back as the second-hop key.
Adds focused tests for deterministic two-step memory readout and sparse hybrid chain schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing normal query readout and chain-k2 readout on full-context and sparse top-4 write schedules.
Reports that chain-k2 improves the multi-hop slice but sharply regresses single-key, multi-key, delayed-query, and aggregate carry.
Records the decision to reject always-on chain-k2 as a default policy and promote a learned halt/continue verifier gate as the next multi-hop mechanism.

tickets.title: Cue-presence conditional chain gate
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_cond_chain_k2 as a sparse-write conditional retrieval-chain schedule.
Propagates exact context content_write_mask data into query evaluation for verifier-style chain decisions.
Implements memory_chain_policy=predicted_written_cue and predicted_token_is_written_cue to continue only when the first memory prediction is also a written cue.
Adds memory_chain_fraction diagnostics to query metrics.
Adds focused tests for direct-value halting, bridge-key continuation, and sparse conditional-chain schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing normal top-4 readout, always-on chain-k2, and cue-presence conditional chain-k2.
Reports that cue-presence conditional chaining repairs most of the always-on direct-recall collapse but still regresses multi-key and fails to improve multi-hop.
Records the decision to reject cue-presence conditional chaining as final and require a learned verifier/halt head with bridge-needed labels next.

tickets.title: Oracle target-path chain positive control
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_oracle_chain_k2 as a sparse-write two-step retrieval-chain positive-control schedule.
Implements memory_chain_policy=oracle_written_target using predicted_token_reaches_written_target over written cue/value pairs and query value targets.
Adds focused tests for oracle target-path detection, direct-value halting, bridge-path continuation, and sparse oracle-chain schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed local matrix comparing full_update, query_skip, plain hybrid top-4, always-on chain-k2, cue-presence conditional chain-k2, and oracle target-path chain-k2.

Reports that the oracle chain keeps context writes at 12.9%, disables query writes, improves aggregate carry over full update by +0.0972, repairs the TAC-106 multi-key blocker, and improves multi-hop over full update.

Records the decision that oracle_written_target is not deployable because it uses target labels, but validates TAC-108: a learned verifier/halt head should approximate the oracle decision without label access.

tickets.title: Learned verifier chain gate
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_learned_chain_k2 as a supervised verifier-gated retrieval-chain schedule.
Implements ChainVerifierGate, train_chain_verifier_gate, chain_verifier_features, apply_chain_verifier_gate, and memory_chain_policy=learned_verifier.
Trains verifier labels from the TAC-107 oracle target-path criterion while restricting inference to label-free first-read/query/context features.
Adds focused tests for verifier feature extraction, gate application, learned query chaining, and sparse learned-chain schedule evaluation.
Runs a multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing learned verifier chaining against full_update, query_skip, plain hybrid top-4, cue-presence conditional chaining, and oracle target-path chaining.
Reports that the learned verifier nearly matches oracle aggregate carry and beats plain hybrid top-4 aggregate carry while keeping 12.9% context writes and no query writes.

Records the blocker that the threshold-0 learned verifier over-continues on multi-hop and underperforms plain hybrid top-4 on that slice, requiring calibration or precision-weighted training before promotion.

tickets.title: Learned verifier threshold sweep
acceptance: Adds learned verifier threshold schedules for thresholds 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0.
Propagates chain_gate_threshold into ChainVerifierGate and learned_verifier query selection.
Adds a focused sparse-schedule test for a thresholded learned verifier schedule.

Runs a multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing thresholded learned verifiers against full_update, query_skip, plain hybrid top-4, oracle target-path chaining, and threshold-0 learned verifier chaining.
Reports that threshold 0.0 remains the best learned aggregate and thresholds 1.0 through 3.0 reduce aggregate carry.
Reports that no static threshold fixes the multi-hop blocker; higher thresholds reduce chain fraction but also filter useful bridge continuations.
Records the decision to reject static threshold calibration and move next to a counterfactual second-read validator.

tickets.title: Counterfactual second-read validator
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_counterfactual_chain_k2 as a sparse-write two-step retrieval-chain schedule.
Implements memory_chain_policy=counterfactual_confidence and counterfactual_second_read_is_better to accept a second read only when confidence, margin, and prediction-change gates pass.
Adds counterfactual_min_confidence_gain and counterfactual_min_margin_gain schedule fields and propagates them through query evaluation.
Adds focused tests for confidence-gain validation, counterfactual query chaining, sparse counterfactual schedule evaluation, and shared write-gate seed groups.
Fixes chain-policy comparison methodology by adding write_gate_seed_group so hybrid top-4 chain variants share the base trained sparse write gate.
Runs an initial counterfactual matrix and records it as superseded because a zero-chain policy still differed from the base schedule due to the write-gate seed confound.

Runs a corrected shared-gate multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing full_update, query_skip, hybrid top-4, oracle chaining, learned chaining, thresholded learned chaining, and counterfactual chaining.
Reports that counterfactual confidence validation is safe but too conservative: it selects zero second reads and exactly matches plain hybrid top-4 carry at 0.3861.

Reports that the corrected oracle target-path chain remains a non-deployable diagnostic ceiling at 0.3944 aggregate carry and 0.0167 chain fraction, while learned verifier variants underperform plain hybrid top-4 on aggregate and multi-hop.

Records the decision to keep hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 as the best current deployable sparse-write baseline and move next to a deployable bridge-target verifier with richer inference-time evidence.

tickets.title: Bridge-target verifier
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_bridge_chain_k2 as a sparse-write bridge-verifier retrieval-chain schedule.
Implements memory_chain_policy=bridge_verifier and bridge_verifier_features with first-read and candidate second-read evidence.
Extends train_chain_verifier_gate so bridge-verifier schedules train a 16-feature gate against oracle target-path labels while using only label-free features at inference.
Adds focused tests for bridge feature exposure, query-time bridge verifier selection, and sparse bridge schedule evaluation.
Runs a corrected shared-gate multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing full_update, query_skip, hybrid top-4, oracle chaining, learned chaining, counterfactual chaining, and bridge-verifier chaining.
Reports that bridge-verifier chaining improves delayed_query to 0.6250 and noisy_key to 0.0764, showing useful second-read signal.

Reports that bridge-verifier chaining is rejected as final because aggregate carry 0.3847 remains below hybrid top-4 0.3861, single_key and multi_key regress, and multi_hop drops to 0.0417 with over-continuation at 0.1111 chain fraction.
Records the decision to keep the bridge feature surface but require TAC-112 to add false-positive cost and direct-recall guards before any chain policy can replace plain hybrid top-4.

tickets.title: Precision-biased bridge gate
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_bridge_precision_chain_k2 as a sparse-write precision-biased bridge-verifier schedule.
Adds chain_gate_positive_weight and chain_gate_negative_weight schedule fields and applies them in train_chain_verifier_gate's weighted BCE objective.
Adds a focused sparse-schedule test confirming the precision bridge schedule uses a negative verifier weight above 1.0.

Runs a corrected shared-gate multi-task, multi-seed matrix comparing full_update, query_skip, hybrid top-4, oracle chaining, counterfactual chaining, unweighted bridge chaining, and precision bridge chaining.
Reports that precision weighting reduces aggregate chain fraction from 0.0431 to 0.0042 but lowers aggregate carry from 0.3847 to 0.3819.
Reports that precision bridge remains below hybrid top-4 on aggregate, preserves single_key and multi_key regressions, loses noisy-key gains, and worsens multi_hop to 0.0347.
Records the decision to reject simple weighted BCE plus thresholding and move next to an explicit direct-answer veto or constrained two-stage chain policy.

tickets.title: Run 5B optimizer precision sanity repair
acceptance: Adds run5b_capability_config and run5b_capability_training_kwargs as a Run 5-comparable preset that preserves p12 base_semantic routing while forcing fp32 by default.
Adds trainer support for --preset run5b_capability without changing explicit user precision overrides.
Adds optimizer-health telemetry that records gradient_norm, precision, grad_scaler_scale, min_gradient_norm, and pass/fail reasons in latest_metrics.
Adds a fail-on-unhealthy-optimization gate for Run 5B so zero-gradient or collapsed fp16 scaler runs fail early instead of producing ambiguous long-run artifacts.
Persists the optimizer-health gate settings in run_manifest.json.
Adds focused tests for Run 5B preset defaults, trainer config selection, and a two-step CPU smoke run with fp32, nonzero gradient telemetry, grad_scaler_scale=1.0, and passing optimizer health.

Records the decision that Run 5B is a local trainer repair, not architecture promotion evidence; the next external gate must complete a capability-plus-specialization run before TAC architecture conclusions are promoted.

tickets.title: Run 5B Kaggle capability launch
acceptance: Builds a fresh Run 5B code dataset from the repaired trainer and presets.
Creates runs/kaggle_run5b_code_2026_06_03 with dataset metadata for mathewlincoln/tac-run5b-capability-code-2026-06-03.
Creates runs/kaggle_run5b_capability_2026_06_03 with a Run 5B kernel wrapper and kernel metadata for mathewlincoln/tac-run-5b-capability-p12-fp32-20k.
Validates Run 5B dataset metadata, kernel metadata, wrapper py_compile, and presence of run5b_capability/optimization_health symbols in the staged code dataset.
Pushes the Run 5B code dataset to Kaggle and verifies its files are visible.
Attempts to push the Run 5B Kaggle GPU kernel.
Blocks on Kaggle weekly GPU quota with: Maximum weekly GPU quota of 30.00 hours reached.

Requires a retry after quota reset; promotion remains blocked until the external Run 5B capability-plus-specialization run completes and clears the nonzero-gradient, ln(512), and final specialization/knockout gates.

tickets.title: Bridge-veto chain policy and verifier-stream audit
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_bridge_veto_chain_k2 as a sparse-write bridge-verifier chain schedule with direct-answer veto.
Adds is_direct_written_answer so bridge continuation is blocked only when the first read is a direct written answer and not itself a written bridge cue.
Adds memory_chain_policy=bridge_verifier_direct_veto to the two-step chained readout path.
Adds focused tests for direct-answer veto semantics, query-time bridge-veto selection, sparse schedule evaluation, and aggregate memory_chain_fraction reporting.
Fixes a verifier-training methodology confound by preserving and restoring ChunkedRecallBatcher RNG state around all sparse-gate and chain-verifier training before evaluation.
Adds a regression test proving gate training does not advance the evaluation batcher stream.
Runs the initial bridge-veto matrix and marks it superseded because verifier training had advanced the shared batcher stream.
Runs a corrected state-preserved local matrix across 5 chunked-recall tasks, 3 seeds, and 8 schedules at 120 training steps.
Reports that bridge-veto, unweighted bridge, precision bridge, counterfactual, oracle, and plain hybrid top-4 all tie at 0.1861 aggregate carry once evaluated on the same stream.
Reports that bridge-veto and unweighted bridge still select continuations (0.0264 aggregate chain fraction) but produce no carry lift over plain hybrid top-4, while precision bridge selects none.
Records the decision to reject direct-answer veto as a balanced solution and to treat earlier learned/verifier chain comparisons as superseded unless rerun with batcher-state preservation.

tickets.title: Sparse graph-path read diagnostic
acceptance: Adds hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4_graph_path_k2 as a sparse-write graph/path readout schedule.
Adds memory_chain_policy=sparse_graph_path and sparse_graph_path_tokens to follow latest written cue/value edges for up to k hops.
Adds focused tests for direct graph lookup, two-hop graph lookup, latest-edge overwrite behavior, query-time graph-path readout, and sparse graph-path schedule evaluation.
Runs a corrected state-preserved local matrix across 5 chunked-recall tasks, 3 seeds, and 7 schedules at 120 training steps.
Reports that graph-path readout ties plain hybrid top-4 at 0.2448 aggregate carry despite a much higher aggregate chain fraction of 0.1969.
Reports that graph-path chains on 0.8958 of multi-hop examples but remains at 0.0208 multi-hop carry, below full_update at 0.0260.
Records the decision to reject deterministic graph-path readout as a balanced solution and to move next toward path-aligned/noise-robust sparse write-gate training.

tickets.title: Path-aligned sparse write gate
acceptance: Adds path_aligned_content_write_mask to supervise direct answer edges, multi-hop bridge edges, and value-ending noisy-key edges.
Adds train_path_aligned_hybrid_write_gate as a sibling hybrid sparse-write gate trained against path-aligned targets.
Adds path_aligned_hybrid_top_4 and path_aligned_hybrid_top_4_graph_path_k2 schedules.
Adds focused tests for path-target construction, learned path-edge recovery, and sparse path-aligned schedule evaluation.
Runs a corrected state-preserved local matrix across 5 chunked-recall tasks, 3 seeds, and 6 schedules at 120 training steps.
Reports that pure path alignment improves multi_hop carry to 0.0365 versus hybrid top-4 0.0208 and full_update 0.0260.
Reports that pure path alignment is rejected because aggregate carry drops to 0.1792 versus hybrid top-4 0.2448, with regressions on multi_key, delayed_query, and noisy_key.
Records the next direction as a mixed structural-plus-path write objective with explicit no-regression gates.

tickets.title: Mixed structural-plus-path sparse write gate
acceptance: Adds mixed_path_structural_content_write_mask as the union of structural boundary targets and path-aligned answer-edge targets.
Adds train_mixed_path_structural_hybrid_write_gate as a sibling hybrid sparse-write gate trained against the mixed target.
Adds mixed_path_structural_hybrid_top_4 and mixed_path_structural_hybrid_top_4_graph_path_k2 schedules.
Adds focused tests for mixed-target construction, learned path-edge preservation, and sparse mixed schedule evaluation.
Runs a corrected state-preserved local matrix across 5 chunked-recall tasks, 3 seeds, and 6 schedules at 120 training steps.
Reports that the mixed OR target is rejected because aggregate carry is 0.1771, below pure path 0.1792, full_update 0.1844, and hybrid top-4 0.2448.
Reports that the mixed target loses the TAC-117 multi-hop improvement, reaching 0.0104 multi-hop carry versus pure path 0.0365 and full_update 0.0260.
Records the next direction as a protected path-edge quota or two-head scorer that preserves the hybrid top-4 backbone instead of retraining one scorer on union targets.

tickets.title: Protected path-quota hybrid sparse write gate
acceptance: Adds ProtectedPathQuotaGate to combine a structural hybrid sparse-write gate with a separate path-aligned sparse-write gate.
Adds protected_path_quota_content_write_mask to reserve one top-4 write slot for a path-scored edge while preserving the remaining slots for hybrid structural scores.
Adds protected_path_quota_hybrid_top_4_q1 and protected_path_quota_hybrid_top_4_q1_graph_path_k2 schedules.
Adds focused tests for path-slot reservation and protected-quota sparse schedule evaluation.
Runs a corrected state-preserved local matrix across 5 chunked-recall tasks, 3 seeds, and 6 schedules at 120 training steps.
Reports that protected path-quota is the best path-integration variant so far on aggregate at 0.2323 carry versus pure path 0.1792 and mixed OR 0.1771.

Reports that protected path-quota is rejected as final because aggregate carry remains below hybrid top-4 0.2448, multi_key falls from 0.4167 to 0.3177, noisy_key remains below hybrid, and multi_hop drops to 0.0156.
Reports that protected graph-path readout chains on 0.2313 of aggregate examples and 0.9740 of multi_hop examples without carry lift.

Records the decision to keep hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 as the best supported local sparse-write prototype and stop treating fixed path quotas, union targets, or graph traversal as standalone A1 repairs.

tickets.title: A1 selector upper-bound audit
acceptance: Uses the TAC-119 corrected matrix to compute a non-deployable oracle task selector over the current A1 fixed sparse-write variants.
Reports that the oracle selector chooses protected path-quota for single_key and delayed_query, hybrid top-4 for multi_key and noisy_key, and pure path-aligned top-4 for multi_hop.
Reports that the selector upper bound is 0.2604 aggregate carry versus hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 at 0.2448, only +0.0156 lift.
Records the decision to close the current local A1 variant loop because the remaining fixed-rule gains are small, fragmented, and non-deployable.

Promotes hybrid_ranked_boundary_top_4 as the best evidence-backed local sparse-write prototype while moving the broader research plan to A2 write-policy integration, A4 speed harnessing, and external Run 5B retry after Kaggle quota reset.

tickets.title: A2 write-policy integration prototype
acceptance: Adds ContentWritePolicy as the public enum for content-memory write behavior.
Adds write_policy support to TACTransformerLM.forward while preserving the existing update_content_memory and content_write_mask compatibility path.
Adds ContentWritePolicy.MASKED_PREFILL_QUERY_SKIP so masked multi-token prefill writes compose with single-token decode/query write skipping.
Exports ContentWritePolicy from tac_transformer.
Adds write_policy metadata and masked_prefill_query_skip to EFFICIENCY_RESEARCH_VARIANTS.
Adds write_policy_from_settings to the research benchmark harness and threads the resolved policy into sequence and decode model calls.

Adds focused tests proving masked prefill through the policy matches explicit content_write_mask behavior, decode skips content-store mutation, invalid policies are rejected, and efficiency variants expose policy metadata.

Runs a 512-token prefill plus 64-token decode synthetic probe proving masked policy prefill matches the explicit sparse path, decode leaves content store and parameters untouched, and identity routing activations remain present.
Records the decision to accept A2 as a local integration prototype and move next to A4 stable speed benchmarking.

tickets.title: A4 local inference benchmark harness
acceptance: Extends kaggle/benchmark_inference.py to report prefill_peak_memory_bytes, carried_query_peak_memory_bytes, and decode_peak_memory_bytes.
Uses torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated for CUDA peak memory and tracemalloc as a dependency-free CPU peak allocation proxy.
Threads ContentWritePolicy.QUERY_SKIP into the benchmark decode path so A4 decode timing uses the A2 write-policy surface.
Updates inference benchmark Markdown output to include mean and per-row prefill/decode peak memory.
Adds focused test coverage for CPU peak-memory reporting.
Runs a compact local CPU baseline comparing vanilla_matched, base_program_memory, content_addressed_k1, and current_best at seq_len 16 and 64.
Reports that current_best has the strongest tested TAC throughput profile with mean prefill vs vanilla 2.7550, carried-query vs vanilla 1.7583, and decode vs vanilla 0.7968.
Reports that decode still trails vanilla on average and therefore supports moving next to A3 identity-state/KV-cache work.
Records the decision that A4 is locally satisfied as a benchmark harness and baseline artifact, not as a final serving-performance claim.

tickets.title: A3 identity decode cache feasibility diagnostic
acceptance: Adds experiments/benchmark_identity_decode_cache.py to profile identity-field decode cost and program assignment stability.
Instruments IdentityFieldLayer.forward during single-token decode with ContentWritePolicy.QUERY_SKIP.
Reports identity-field decode time fraction, theoretical identity-cache speedup ceiling, identity-field calls per iteration, and program switch fraction.
Adds focused tests for identity-cache speedup-ceiling and switch-rate summarization.
Runs a compact local CPU diagnostic at seq_len 64, decode_steps 64, batch_size 2, and 3 iterations.
Reports identity-field fraction 0.5802, speedup ceiling 2.3820x, and program switch fraction 0.8665.
Rejects a naive whole-identity-output cache because high program-switch rate means a reuse-until-switch guard would invalidate almost every step.
Records the next A3 implementation direction as an exact lower-level state-maintenance bypass that keeps token-dependent routing fresh while avoiding content-store maintenance under QUERY_SKIP.

tickets.title: A3 exact decode state-maintenance bypass
acceptance: Adds ContentWritePolicy.DECODE_STATE_SKIP as an explicit serving/decode policy distinct from QUERY_SKIP.
Keeps dense state updates for multi-token prefill while skipping identity-state maintenance during single-token decode.
Skips program-memory writes, allocation, reconsolidation, memory-tier updates, age/frequency updates, engram updates, and content-store updates during decode_state_skip single-token decode.
Preserves incoming recurrent identity state exactly, including optional None fields, while still computing fresh token-dependent routing and program context for logits.
Adds focused tests proving first-token decode logits match QUERY_SKIP and returned recurrent state remains unchanged.
Updates the identity decode benchmark to compare query_skip and decode_state_skip policies side by side.
Runs a compact local CPU profile reporting query_skip at 317.89 tok/s and decode_state_skip at 417.77 tok/s with unchanged program switch fraction 0.8665.
Records the decision to accept decode_state_skip as the current exact A3 serving prototype while continuing to reject approximate whole-output identity caching without a stronger drift guard.

tickets.title: A4 decode-policy inference harness integration
acceptance: Adds --decode-policies to kaggle/benchmark_inference.py so TAC decode write policies can be compared in the main inference harness.
Adds row-level decode_policy and profile labels, using variant:policy summary keys for TAC rows while keeping vanilla as the baseline profile.
Ensures decode_vs_base compares each TAC row against the BASE row with the same decode policy.
Updates Markdown output to include decode policy in row tables.
Adds focused tests for decode-policy parsing and profile labeling.
Runs a compact local CPU A4 matrix comparing vanilla_matched, base_program_memory, and current_best under query_skip and decode_state_skip.
Reports that current_best improves from mean decode_vs_vanilla 0.1606 under query_skip to 0.1980 under decode_state_skip, and from mean decode_vs_base 0.7522 to 1.0815.
Reports that base_program_memory regresses under decode_state_skip, so decode_state_skip remains an opt-in current-best serving candidate rather than a universal TAC default.

tickets.title: B2 route-and-reconstruct diagnostic
acceptance: Adds experiments/benchmark_route_reconstruct_diagnostic.py as a standalone B2 diagnostic.
Trains a compact TAC plus route decoder with a light route-reconstruction objective on the prepared local corpus.
Computes counterfactual hidden reconstruction loss for the routed program and every alternative program.
Reports routed-is-best fraction, routed-minus-best loss, other-minus-routed loss, best-program margin, and route/program gradient norms.
Adds focused tests for counterfactual route reconstruction statistics and diagnostic verdict classification.
Runs a compact local CPU diagnostic and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/route_reconstruct_diagnostic_local_2026_06_03.
Reports routed_is_best_fraction 0.0967, mean_routed_minus_best 0.0920, mean_other_minus_routed -0.0152, and max_route_grad_norm 0.006445.
Records the decision that B2 points to functional route misalignment rather than dead gradient flow, so B1 should test utility-aligned hard-negative and temperature-annealed routing losses.

tickets.title: B1 program contrastive refinement
acceptance: Adds experiments/benchmark_program_contrastive_refinement.py with route_reconstruct_reference, hard_negative, task_conditioned, and annealed_utility variants.
Uses counterfactual reconstruction losses to derive utility-aligned best-program labels for hard-negative, task-conditioned, and temperature-annealed objectives.
Reports program-memory cosine, raw program-embedding cosine, routed-is-best fraction, and routed-minus-best reconstruction gap.
Adds focused tests for hard-negative loss behavior, annealed temperature, and B1 row summarization.
Runs a compact local CPU matrix and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/program_contrastive_refinement_local_2026_06_03.
Reports that task_conditioned is the best routing-alignment variant, improving routed_is_best_fraction to 0.2754 and routed-minus-best gap to 0.0522.
Reports that all variants leave program_memory_cosine at 0.9167 and therefore miss the pasted B1 success criterion of below 0.85.
Records the decision to reject loss-only B1 promotion and move next to direct program-memory diversification while keeping task_conditioned as the best route-alignment auxiliary candidate.

tickets.title: Program-memory separation probe
acceptance: Extends experiments/benchmark_program_contrastive_refinement.py with task_conditioned_memsep_0p1 and task_conditioned_memsep_1p0 variants.
Adds output.aux.losses['separation'] to the training objective for direct program-memory separation rows.
Keeps the B1 gate tied to program-memory cosine rather than raw program-embedding cosine.
Adds focused test coverage that the direct-memory-separation variants are present.
Runs an expanded compact local CPU matrix and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/program_contrastive_memsep_local_2026_06_03.
Reports that task_conditioned_memsep_0p1 is the strongest route-utility candidate so far, with routed_is_best_fraction 0.4502 and routed-best gap 0.0400.
Reports that program_memory_cosine remains 0.9167 for every row, so direct loss-weight tuning still fails the below-0.85 B1 gate.
Records the decision that the next Track B step must instrument and alter the program-memory write/allocation path rather than adding more objective-only variants.

tickets.title: Program-memory write/allocation diagnostic
acceptance: Adds experiments/benchmark_program_memory_write_diagnostic.py as a Track B diagnostic for program-memory write coverage and update collapse.
Compares stability_task_memsep, creb_k1_task_memsep, and creb_k2_task_memsep allocation/write variants under the best current task-conditioned memory-separation auxiliary.

Reports program-memory cosine, dead-program fraction, selected load entropy/gini, write-frequency entropy/gini, memory norm distribution, age distribution, and per-program selected/write/norm vectors.
Adds focused tests for entropy/gini summarization, dead-program classification, and refusing to recommend low-cosine rows that are only low because programs are dead.
Runs a compact local CPU matrix and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/program_memory_write_diagnostic_local_2026_06_03.
Reports that stability_task_memsep writes broadly with write_frequency_entropy 1.0000 and dead_program_fraction 0.0000 but remains collapsed at program_memory_cosine 0.9167.
Reports that creb_k1_task_memsep and creb_k2_task_memsep reduce cosine only by leaving dead_program_fraction 0.6667 and 0.5417, so no row is recommended.
Records the decision that the next Track B prototype should make candidate_memory program-specific instead of adding more loss weight or sparse allocation alone.

tickets.title: Program-conditioned memory update prototype
acceptance: Adds TACConfig.program_memory_update_type with shared default behavior and an opt-in program_conditioned update path.
Implements program-conditioned candidate_memory by combining pooled hidden state with each program embedding through a trainable update projection.
Preserves existing default behavior and parameter accounting for shared program-memory updates.
Updates estimate_tac_parameter_count for the opt-in program-conditioned update projection.
Adds focused tests for invalid program_memory_update_type rejection, per-program candidate diversity, gradient flow, and parameter accounting.
Extends experiments/benchmark_program_memory_write_diagnostic.py with program-conditioned stability and CREB top-k variants.
Tightens write-diagnostic viability so low-cosine rows are not recommended unless dead_program_fraction is below 0.2.
Runs a compact local write/allocation sweep and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/program_conditioned_memory_budget_sweep_local_2026_06_03.
Reports that program_conditioned_creb_k6_task_memsep reaches program_memory_cosine 0.2064, dead_program_fraction 0.0833, and write_frequency_entropy 0.9294.
Extends experiments/benchmark_program_contrastive_refinement.py with program_conditioned_creb_k6_task_memsep.
Runs a compact local B1 balance head-to-head and records the artifact in runs/benchmarks/program_conditioned_b1_balance_local_2026_06_03.
Reports that program_conditioned_creb_k6_task_memsep passes the local B1 gate with program_memory_cosine 0.2057 and routed_is_best_fraction 0.3359.
Records the decision to promote program_conditioned_creb_k6_task_memsep as the current balanced local Track B prototype, pending longer local stability and Run 5B external validation.

The 80-step result is identity_share=0.3274 and identity_to_transformer=0.4867, with p24 reaching stronger specialization and lower memory cosine but not enough final-loss or throughput advantage to win locally.

tickets.title: Local TAC energy plus compression valuation matrix

tickets.title: Identity compression phase-boundary benchmark
acceptance: Builds a TAC phase-boundary benchmark that measures identity retention after distractor task counts N=0,5,10,20,50.
Sweeps activation_l1 compression strength across at least four values including zero while holding the energy-training objective fixed.

Reports identity retention, LM accuracy, energy pair accuracy, rerank accuracy, routing entropy, activation density, active-program fraction, compute energy, and compression score for each compression strength and seed.
Identifies the compression breakpoint where compactness continues improving but identity retention begins to fall.
Uses at least three seeds before making a compression phase-boundary claim.
Writes JSON and Markdown artifacts under a new runs/benchmarks identity-compression phase-boundary directory.
Records the boundary that TAC-202 is preliminary and does not by itself prove compact persistent identity representations.

Current result: added experiments/benchmark_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py and tests_py/test_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py. Ran a 3-seed 500-step sweep over strengths 0.0,0.01,0.03,0.05,0.10, then extended with 0.2,0.4,0.8 and combined the saved rows under runs/benchmarks/identity_compression_phase_boundary_combined_2026_06_07. The combined artifact maps an identity compression phase boundary at activation_l1 strength 0.2. Retention peaks at strength 0.05 with identity_retention_mean=0.5917 and compression_score=0.4389. At strength 0.2, retention falls to 0.3667 while compression improves to 0.5002, crossing the 0.10 retention-drop threshold. Energy accuracy stays high at 0.9531 and rerank accuracy is 0.8750 at the boundary, so the failure mode is specifically identity retention rather than energy learning collapse.

tickets.title: Fine-grained identity compression critical-threshold fit

Reports identity retention, activation density, routing entropy, active-program fraction, compression score, LM accuracy, energy pair accuracy, and rerank accuracy for every strength and seed.
Fits identity-retention and activation-density curves and estimates the critical compression threshold with an uncertainty interval instead of only naming the first crossed grid point.
Confirms whether the TAC-203 coarse boundary near activation_l1 0.20 should be refined to a lower threshold around the 0.08-0.18 region.
Updates research.md with the fitted critical threshold and states whether the supported claim is an operating-point recommendation, a phase-boundary result, or both.

Current result: added a fitted critical-threshold estimator to experiments/benchmark_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py and focused tests in tests_py/test_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py. Best identity retention remains at activation_l1 0.05 with identity_retention_mean=0.6300, compression_score=0.4442, density=0.3223, energy_pair=0.9656, rerank=0.9063, and LM=0.8454. At activation_l1 0.125, retention falls to 0.4000 while compression improves to 0.4863, density falls to 0.2163, energy_pair remains 0.9469, rerank remains 0.8875, and LM remains 0.8454. tickets.title: Larger-model identity compression boundary validation
acceptance: Runs a larger TAC identity-compression validation using d_model=48, n_layers=2, n_programs=8, and energy_budget=4.0.

tickets.title: Routing-structure mechanism test for identity compression boundary
acceptance: Adds route-structure diagnostics to the identity-compression benchmark: per-program route utilization, activation utilization, effective program count, top-program share, load standard deviation, and zero-utilization fraction.
Runs the larger TAC-205 configuration across activation_l1 strengths 0.0,0.05,0.10,0.125 with at least three seeds and 500 steps per cell.
Reports identity retention, energy pair accuracy, rerank accuracy, LM accuracy, activation density, routing entropy, and the new routing-structure metrics together.
Determines whether identity degradation near the boundary coincides with route simplification, route concentration, or loss of program-utilization diversity.
Writes JSON and Markdown artifacts under a new runs/benchmarks routing-mechanism directory.
Updates research.md and progress.txt with the mechanism interpretation before changing any default compression recommendation.

Current result: added routing_structure_metrics to experiments/benchmark_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py and focused test coverage in tests_py/test_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py. Ran the TAC-205 larger configuration under runs/benchmarks/identity_compression_route_mechanism_tac206_2026_06_07. The fitted critical strength remains 0.1098 with grid crossing 0.125. Identity retention stays 0.5278 at activation_l1 0.05 while activation density falls from 0.4611 to 0.1664 and route effective programs slightly improves from 7.2669 to 7.3553. At activation_l1 0.125, identity retention falls to 0.3500 while energy/rerank/LM remain 0.9844/0.9271/0.9250. The boundary does not look like a single-program collapse: route top1 share stays near 0.20 and activation effective programs stay near 7.75-7.91 out of 8. It does show selected-route diversity erosion: route effective programs fall from 7.3553 at 0.05 to 6.7567 at 0.125, route load std rises from 0.1751 to 0.2280, and route underused fraction rises from 0.0729 to 0.1667. Mechanism interpretation: identity failure is driven by activation-density collapse plus mild selected-route diversity erosion, while energy/ranking survives much thinner activations.

tickets.title: Representational thinning mechanism test
acceptance: Adds representation-thinning diagnostics to the identity-compression benchmark: selected activation norm, identity-state norm, selected identity-state norm, energy-feature norm, and marker-identity information probes.

Current result: added representational_thinning_metrics and marker-information probes to experiments/benchmark_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py with focused tests in tests_py/test_identity_compression_phase_boundary.py. Ran the larger TAC-205/TAC-206 setup under runs/benchmarks/identity_compression_representation_thinning_tac207_2026_06_07. The grid crossing remains activation_l1 0.125 and fitted critical strength remains 0.1098. Selected activation mean falls from 0.600 at baseline to 0.234 at 0.05, 0.102 at 0.10, and 0.078 at 0.125. Selected identity-state norm falls from 0.267 to 0.131, 0.062, and 0.054. Identity-state probe MI fraction falls from 0.629 to 0.446, 0.326, and 0.287. Energy-feature norm remains stable around 3.64-3.70 and energy-feature probe MI remains comparatively stable at 0.472, 0.397, 0.509, and 0.462 while energy pair accuracy reaches/stays 0.984 above 0.05. tickets.title: Identity thinning causal rescue experiment
acceptance: Runs the activation_l1=0.125 identity-failing regime while keeping sparsity pressure unchanged.
Compares a compressed control against auxiliary rescue variants that preserve identity-state magnitude, marker-identity information, or both.
Uses the larger TAC-205/TAC-206/TAC-207 local configuration with at least three seeds and 500 steps per variant/seed cell.

Reports identity retention, compression score, activation density, selected activation, selected identity-state norm, identity probe MI, energy feature MI, energy pair accuracy, rerank accuracy, and LM accuracy.
Counts a rescue as supported only if identity retention improves materially while compression, energy, rerank, and LM quality remain comparable to the compressed control.
Writes JSON and Markdown artifacts under a new runs/benchmarks identity-thinning-rescue directory.
Updates research.md and progress.txt with whether restoring identity-state mass/information restores identity retention.

Current result: added experiments/benchmark_identity_thinning_rescue.py and tests_py/test_identity_thinning_rescue.py. Ran the aligned 4-variant, 3-seed, 500-step rescue sweep under runs/benchmarks/identity_thinning_rescue_tac209_2026_06_07 at activation_l1=0.125. The compressed control reproduces the failing regime with identity_retention_mean=0.3500, compression_score=0.5687, selected_identity_state_norm=0.0536, energy_pair=0.9844, rerank=0.9271, and LM=0.9250. Norm-floor rescue wins with identity_retention_mean=0.5500, compression_score=0.5608, selected_identity_state_norm=0.1883, energy_pair=0.9635, rerank=0.9583, and LM=0.9299. Retention gain is +0.2000 with only -0.0079 compression delta, -0.0208 energy delta, and +0.0313 rerank delta. Marker-info and combined rescues greatly increase probe MI but do not restore retention as well, so the supported causal claim is that preserving selected identity-state magnitude rescues functional identity retention, while auxiliary marker probe information alone is not sufficient.
